nodes.label: American Red Cross Club at The University of Texas at Dallas

meta.description: Red Cross UTD is dedicated to promoting awareness of the humanitarian goals of the American Red Cross by participating as a student chapter in the DFW Metroplex. We aim to contribute to the prevention and alleviation of suffering in the face of disaster while upholding the American Red Cross Code of Conduct and the seven Fundamental Principles of the International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies.

We are a group of students excited to bridge the gap between medicine and technology, creating a vibrant platform for students passionate about both fields to come together. Additionally, not only do we want to create a platform from which medical and technology based students can leap off of, but also one where students from a diverse array of academic backgrounds can learn and develop an interest in the fields. Our mission is to foster innovation, interdisciplinary collaboration, and the exchange of knowledge among students interested in the intersection of medicine and technology. In other words: to bring students from these two very different but intersecting fields together in their quest for knowledge. We plan to facilitate this through a variety of events, from guest speakers in the field to relevant workshops, hackathons, and much much more.

The Artificial Intelligence Society is one of the largest computer science organizations at the University of Texas at Dallas. We are a team of passionate individuals, devoted to bridging the gap between people's awareness and the world of AI. Our mission is to promote artificial intelligence education at UT Dallas and in the surrounding communities. We have placed educational outreach as one of our main priorities, to inspire others to learn more about AI past the resources our organization provides. Regardless of age, major, or experience, we believe that a simple appreciation of artificial intelligence will become necessary as this technology advances. If you stick with us, we promise to replace your fear with passion, and complexity with simplicity. You can join us at one of our several events throughout the semester, including AI Chats: where we bring together professors, professionals, and peers into a conversational panel, ML Monday: where we host informative lectures regarding the world of AI. For more information please visit our website Follow our Instagram for Updates

ASCM UT Dallas Student Forum mission is to enhance our member's experience outside of the classroom on their journey to becoming excellent supply chain professionals. We plan on doing this by providing our members valuable insights through networking opportunities, knowledge on current supply chain trends, providing knowledge regarding ASCM certifications, and professional development opportunities from events hosted by ASCM UT Dallas Student Forum.

We're a team of students with one goal: building a greater, more collaborative computing community at The University of Texas at Dallas. We offer several semester-long programs to help UTD students hone their computer science skills including Projects, Research, Mentor Program, and Technical Interview Prep (TIP). We also have teams that work with our sponsors, team-bonding events, create our marketing, and run HackUTD, the largest hackathon in Texas and the central time zone.

The Association for Information Systems (AIS) is a student organization focused on advancing knowledge and excellence in information systems. As part of a global professional community, AIS supports learning, professional development, and collaboration while exploring how information systems serve organizations and society. Members gain opportunities to grow their skills, connect with peers, and engage with real-world technology.

Our mission is to bring awareness of autoimmune disorders and their varieties to the UT Dallas community! We do this by conducting literary research and by sharing our findings at our fun, but informational meetings. Keep an eye out for our social media posts where we provide bite-sized breakdowns of our research and highlight key figures in the autoimmune research and advocacy. We also contact individuals with certain autoimmune conditions to talk about their experiences as guest speakers or interviewees to receive insight on their journeys.

AWS Organization is a student organization for IT and engineering students at The University of Texas at Dallas. The organization aims to raise awareness of the AWS cloud computing technology and engage in an in-depth discussion of a wide range of topics.

nodes.label: Collegiate Chapter of the Association of Fundraising Professionals at UT Dallas (AFP UTD)

meta.description: Collegiate Chapter of the Association of Fundraising Professionals at UT Dallas (AFP UTD) is the first student-led organization at UT Dallas for students and young professionals who are interested in fundraising. The UTD AFP Collegiate Chapter is also part of something much bigger - a worldwide professional organization that not only is a part of a global network of fundraising professionals across the globe, but also a catalyst for an amazing career. The Chapter is supported by a faculty advisor (Dr. Elizabeth Searing), a local AFP chapter Professional Advisor (Dr. Holly Miori), and a UTD PNM student/Collegiate Chapter President (Thanh Hoang). AFP UTD collegiate chapter will: - Connect students and young professionals sharing passion in nonprofit management and fundraising - Provide learning opportunities such as webinar, fundraising conference, internship and other training opportunities - Prepare students for future career through mentoring, leadership opportunity, and wide network

Our mission is to empower students with knowledge and insights into progressive politics, fostering an environment that encourages learning and critical thinking. Additionally, we aim to utilize our club as a platform for enhancing voter education, promoting informed and engaged citizenship.

Our mission as "Faces of African Muslims" is to create a safe space and platform for Muslims of African descent. We aim to promote awareness and enable the spiritual growth of members with the end goal of cultivating unity within the Muslim community at UTD. We hope to achieve this by hosting many events throughout the year that are educational, engaging, and events that help build bonds for our members. Students and faculty of UTD are all invited no matter what ethnicity or religion you are, come and be part of the FAM!!!

nodes.label: Friends of MSF at UT Dallas

meta.description: Description: The Friends of Médecins Sans Frontières (MSF) is a student-led organization dedicated to advocacy, education, and fundraising. Our chapter works to raise awareness over global medical and humanitarian efforts by engaging the UTD campus in meaningful discussion and initiative. ​Our mission is to prove that even the smallest act of compassion can make a huge difference when we all come together. Purpose: Our goal is not limited to supporting MSF in the present but to build a strong foundation that will allow this organization to thrive for years to come. The current officers are primarily students majoring in the medical field, but we welcome individuals from all disciplines who are passionate about global health and humanitarian work. We believe in fostering an open and welcoming environment, and are always ready to answer questions, offer support, or engage in meaningful discussions.

Our mission is to empower volunteers and under-resourced communities to resolve global health and economic disparities and inspire all involved to collaboratively work towards an equal world. Global Brigades at the University of Texas at Dallas is a secular, international student-operated volunteer organization whose mission is to provide sustainable development to underserved communities throughout the world. It will offer 1 Brigade Program: Medical/Dental. Along with affiliate groups at other distinguished universities, our University of Texas at Dallas team recruits student volunteers and professionals, gathers supplies, and then travels overseas to set up free medical and dental clinics and run educational programs to improve public health and sanitation, in a variety of underserved global communities. Students will visit several community projects that aim to also better the quality of life in these impoverished areas.

nodes.label: Investments and Securities Club at UTD

meta.description: Investing is a crucial tool that not only serves as a great educational experience but as a major method of financial mobility, an opportunity often overlooked for students in college. IMC aims to provide UT Dallas with a community for students interested in learning about investing to come together, increasing our local community's financial literacy.

Mission: "Building community through service" Purpose: To be a beacon of service for the UTD student population. Organization Description: Islamic Relief at UTD (IRUTD for short) is a service-based organization with the aim of providing relief and aid to both our neighboring communities and our family abroad. We work to empower individuals in our community and give them a voice, regardless of gender, race, ethnicity, or religion. Although we draw our inspiration to do service from what Allah has said in the Quran and the teachings of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him), membership is open to anybody committed to putting service to others over themselves.

Marathi Mitra Mandal (MMM) is a student organization dedicated to celebrating and promoting Marathi culture, language, and traditions while building a strong and welcoming community on campus. Our mission is to create a home away from home for students who share an interest in Maharashtra’s rich heritage — from festivals, food, and music to history, art, and community values. We host cultural events such as Gudi Padwa, Diwali, Dasara, and Maharashtra Day, along with social gatherings, game nights, collaborative campus events, and networking opportunities. MMM is open to everyone! Marathi-speaking students, those with Maharashtrian roots, and anyone curious about learning more about our culture. Whether you come for the food, the dhol tasha, or the friendships, you’ll find a supportive and vibrant community here.

nodes.label: Medicine Education and Development for Low Income Families Everywhere (MEDLIFE)

meta.description: MEDLIFE is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization that partners with low-income communities in Latin America and Africa to improve their access to medicine, education, and community development projects. Our ultimate goal is to one day reach a world free from the constraints of poverty by empowering students and volunteers to become advocates for change by becoming leaders in our chapter and supporting the Moving Mountains giving program. MEDLIFE national hires and trains local staff to provide high quality 24/7/365 patient care, community development projects, and educational workshops to empower communities in need. These initiatives would not be possible without forming chapters at universities and high schools across the globe. MEDLIFE Chapters are the pillars of the movement. Our chapter would represent and promote MEDLIFE at UT Dallas and in our local communities. We would make an immeasurable impact - raising money for MEDLIFE, volunteering in Service Learning Trips, engaging in local community service initiatives, and more. We will work hand in hand with low-income communities and student groups to bring healthcare, education, and safe homes to the sites where we work, abroad or locally. The year-round effort will translate into hundreds of thousands of healthier families, better-informed individuals, and safer communities across Latin America, Africa and here in the DFW area. Overall, we aim to involve students, develop leadership skills, have the opportunity to make a difference, and build important connections that last a lifetime.

Our organization exists to support, connect, and empower nontraditional students at UTD by fostering community, sharing resources, and providing a space for members to find commonality in their unique experiences. We aim to support personal, academic, and professional growth for all members.

nodes.label: Partners In Health Engage at the University of Texas at Dallas

meta.description: Partners In Health Engage at UTD is part of a network of teams around the U.S. joining forces to advance the movement for universal health care. We believe that all individuals, no matter where they live, have a fundamental economic, social, and cultural right to a universal standard of health. By working in collaboration with Partners In Health, a global health and social justice nonprofit that provides care in some of the world's most impoverished places, we aim to bring attention to the global disparity in health care access and to push for global change. We call our Congressional representatives, educate our peers, share resources, and raise funds, all in support of the right to health movement. We believe that together, we can build a more just and equitable world in which everyone has access to the care they need. Join us and help build the movement.

The Process Automation and Reengineering Club (PARC) aspires to elicit the interest of individuals in understanding Business Processes, Digital Transformation methodologies, Low Code, and related automation frameworks. We aim to equip the members with the best possible low code tools to identify the gaps in any process, mitigate the effects, and automate any Business Process in an Enterprise.

nodes.label: Queer and Trans People of Color at UT Dallas

meta.description: Mission: To Promote the Herstory, Academic Success, Health, Civic Engagement, and Empowerment of all queer and trans people of color Purpose/Description: To provide a safe social environment to express the varying intersectional identities that come with being a queer and/or trans person of color. We strive to promote the History, Academic Success, Health, Civic Engagement, and Empowerment of all Queer and Trans People Of Color as well as to create a space for chosen families to foster specfically for queer and/or trans students of color who may not be fully embraced in all of their identities.

Roundnet at UT Dallas is a club that seeks to get more students active on campus and interested in this sport (often called spikeball). Our aim is to encompass casual and competitive play so that everyone is welcome, challenged, and able to reach their social and skill level goals.

nodes.label: SAMPE at UTD (Society for the Advancement of Material and Process Engineering at UTD)

meta.description: We are a group of engineering students who are interested in advanced materials and processing technology. Our purpose is to utilize SAMPE’s resources to connect like-minded students with industry professionals and experts, and to bridge the gap between composite design and its applications in manufacturing. This organization falls under the educational/engineering category.

nodes.label: Student Tutors Reviving Identity through Volunteerism and Education

meta.description: STRIVE was founded in 2019 to help underprivileged students in the DFW metroplex have the chance to live up to their true potential. Our initiative first started as a means for Refugee and ESL students to work with select mentors. Our work focused on helping the students overcome language barriers, learn that diversity is indeed accepted, and to think beyond primary education. This initiative was a success as we had a lot of students interested in higher education after various discussions. Unfortunately, many of them struggled to gain acceptance due to the compounding effects of the language barrier. Without a solid grasp of English, performance in various other subjects takes a significant hit. Based on this dilemma, we decided that it was essential to develop a tutoring program for these students to connect them with tutor-mentors from similar backgrounds who would guide the students and allow them to overcome this barrier. We see a bright future for these students, and we want to tap into their potential by providing them with one-on-one subject strategy training and educational advice. Our motto is that every student should have the right to succeed regardless of their background. Language should not be an obstacle to success. We currently work with Lake Highlands High School in the Richardson Independent School District (RISD). Currently looking to expand, we look forward to continuing our mission to eliminate educational disparities.

nodes.label: Students Promoting Acts of Random Kindness (SPARK)

meta.description: Students Promoting Acts of Random Kindness, or SPARK, aims to bring kindness to UTDallas both on and off-campus through volunteering events. We want to connect students to both their peers and their community by combining service with social events to make meaningful changes and memories. SPARK requires no membership dues and no minimum attendance rules. We only require good vibes and a positive spirit!

nodes.label: The Biomedical Professionals Society at UTD

meta.description: The Biomedical Professionals Society at UTD addresses a critical gap in undergraduate biomedical education by creating an organization specifically focused on the intersection of basic research and clinical application. BPS provides comprehensive support for students pursuing diverse paths in biomedical sciences, from academic research to biotechnology entrepreneurship. This organization falls under the educational and professional development categories and serves to bridge industry-academia connections through research-oriented professional development and comprehensive career pathway support (PhD, MD/PhD, industry, entrepreneurship).

ILM is the premier tech, innovation, and entrepreneurial network rooted in faith at UT Dallas. Our mission is to create a space where innovation meets values — connecting students across disciplines to learn, build, and lead together. Through startup projects, networking opportunities, and the founders pipeline, ILM equips members with the skills and community support to thrive in their careers while making a lasting impact. Whether you’re a programmer, entrepreneur, or creative, ILM is designed to help you grow and bring your ideas to life.

nodes.label: The Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers at the University of Texas at Dallas

meta.description: ## ⚡ WHAT IS IEEE UTD? ⚡ The Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers at the University of Texas at Dallas (IEEE UTD) is **the official student chapter of IEEE, the world’s largest professional organization dedicated to advancing technology for the benefit of humanity**. --- ## 🤔 WHAT DOES IEEE UTD DO? 🤔 Our student chapter is a community of engineers, computer scientists, innovators, and problem-solvers who come together to learn, collaborate, and grow through hands-on projects, technical workshops, networking events, and professional development opportunities. As an active student organization, **IEEE UTD offers:** - **Workshops** on circuits, coding, robotics, and more - **Project teams** and technical collaborations (beginner-friendly *and* advanced) to gain hands-on experience and boost resumes - **Tutoring and exam reviews** personalized each semester to the courses students are currently enrolled in at UTD - **Networking** events with peers, industry professionals, and alumni - **Professional development** opportunities and soft skill seminars (interview prep, resume building, teamwork) - **Social and outreach programs** to connect with the engineering community - **Industry tours** with leading companies for exclusive insights into the field - A supportive **community** to build connections and grow together **AND MUCH MORE! 💙** Membership is open to **all UTD students**! No specific major, national IEEE membership, or experience required. --- ## 💡 WHY JOIN IEEE UTD? 💡 IEEE UTD gives you real opportunities to apply what you learn in class through hands-on projects, technical workshops, and competitions, all while building skills that matter beyond the classroom. You’ll grow your resume, expand your network, and find a community that supports your academic and professional journey. Whether you’re just getting started or already experienced, IEEE UTD meets you where you are. **Welcome to IEEE UTD! Let’s engineer the future, together! ⚡**

nodes.label: The National Society of Leadership and Success (NSLS)

meta.description: The mission of the NSLS is "building leaders who make a better world". The NSLS is a student-led honor society with over 800 campuses involved across the US. The board will walk members through the "Foundations of Leadership" program where they learn to set goals and build leadership skills.

Youth With A Mission (YWAM) is a student organization at The University of Texas at Dallas dedicated to supporting international students as they navigate life in the United States. Our mission is to share Christ’s love by creating a welcoming, family-like community where students can grow spiritually, socially, and academically. YWAM provides mentorship, friendship, and practical support to help international students overcome cultural, academic, and personal challenges while building a strong sense of belonging. Through fellowship, prayer, service, teaching, and social activities, we aim to walk alongside students with compassion and care, offering encouragement and guidance every step of the way. Membership is open to all UT Dallas students, faculty, and staff, regardless of background. We value love, faith, inclusivity, and service, and we strive to be a home away from home for those far from their own families.
